title: British TV at its Best: A review of Adolescence
text: Adolescence received exceptional reviews for being some of the best British television in recent years. The show became the first streaming program to top Britain's weekly TV ratings after its first episode was watched by 6.45 million people in the first week of release. The entire series premiered on 13 March 2025 on Netflix. Netflix made the one-shot show Adolescence available for free viewing in UK secondary schools after Prime Minister Keir Starmer's backing, recognizing its cultural significance. Every episode is shot in one continuous take, letting viewers become immersed from the very first moments as events unfold in real time. The show addresses critical issues such as toxic masculinity, social media's impact on youth, and the disconnect between parents and children. The show received thirteen nominations at the 77th Primetime Emmy Awards, winning nine, including Outstanding Limited or Anthology Series, Outstanding Directing, and Outstanding Writing, in addition to acting wins for Lead Actor, Supporting Actor, and Supporting Actress. This critical and commercial success demonstrated the appeal of innovative British television that tackles contemporary social issues.
